notes: Combined run of steps 7.2 and 7.3. (7.2) AppEnvironment.live() now constructs the production ClipboardService() and injects it for both DEBUG and RELEASE wirings; preview() continues to wire the in-process NoopClipboard double so previews/tests never touch NSPasteboard. Wiring is guarded by #if canImport(AppKit); a non-AppKit fallback to UnavailableClipboard keeps the surface compilable. Added KizbaTests/AppEnvironmentClipboardTests.swift with three assertions: live().clipboard is ClipboardService, preview().clipboard is not ClipboardService, and the two factories produce distinct clipboard implementations. No clipboard methods are invoked in the tests. (7.3) EntryDetailModel already exposed copy(_:clearAfterSeconds:), copyPassword(clearAfterSeconds:), and copyMetadata(forKey:clearAfterSeconds:) from earlier phases; the original spec sigature copy(field:clearAfter:) was preserved as the existing copy(_:clearAfterSeconds:) to avoid touching unrelated call-sites and tests (minimal scope). EntryDetailView's existing Copy buttons (password + per-metadata-row) gained accessibility identifiers 'copy-password-button' and 'copy-meta-<index>-button' for future UI tests. Added KizbaTests/EntryDetailModelCopyTests.swift with two model-level tests: a direct copy() forwarding assertion and a copyPassword() round-trip through StubPassManager into .loaded state. Both confirm the value is forwarded verbatim to ClipboardServicing with the expected Duration. project.pbxproj unchanged (PBXFileSystemSynchronizedRootGroup auto-picks new files). Full suite: 178 tests, 0 failures.
commits: test(app): add AppEnvironment clipboard wiring tests
feat(ui): wire Copy buttons in EntryDetailView to EntryDetailModel/ClipboardService
